title: Timeline on my Website Tour - Stoned

enrichment.summary: Kyle tours his personal history timeline website, a multi-section project spanning 15 billion years into the past to 52,000 years into the future, organized into six interlinked sections (prehistory, BC, CE, a 3000-year dense section, and future). He explains its design philosophy: five-column pages for visual and mental symmetry, 12 exponentially-scaling time dimensions, 99 color-and-font-coded event types (11 categories × 9 causes), and UN subregion navigation. He argues that a complete, condensed timeline makes it possible for anyone to become 'history literate' and predict current events by recognizing historical patterns.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: knowing the full scope and outer boundaries of a subject makes it feel smaller and easier to learn
five columns is the perfect number for lists — visually and mentally symmetrical, best use of page width
people who know history can identify patterns in current events and make better decisions
people learn better when they can skip around freely, so a complete navigable timeline beats a linear book
prehistory must scale exponentially because human knowledge of the past decreases exponentially the further back you go
